title: [Bug]: podman image scp fails with confusing error messages
body:
### Issue Description

If I run this:

```
podman image scp my_image:latest user@other_host::
```

It says this:

```
WARN[0000] Unknown connection name given. Please use system connection add to specify the default remote socket location 
Copying blob b12f34769c85 done  
Copying blob 7b3934b1b555 done  
Copying blob 9e0f46b6d29d done  
Copying blob 2e4e862242a9 done  
Copying blob ab61ffc4e8a6 done  
Copying blob 2cb8da93f979 done  
Copying blob 6517fb3ffeb2 done  
Copying config 96ae127d36 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Error: failed to connect: dial tcp: address other_host: missing port in address
```

Ok weird. Maybe it's some parsing bug to do with the `:`. I tried this:

```
podman image scp my_image:latest user@other_host
```

Seems to go a bit better:

```
Copying blob b12f34769c85 done  
Copying blob 7b3934b1b555 done  
Copying blob 9e0f46b6d29d done  
Copying blob 2e4e862242a9 done  
Copying blob ab61ffc4e8a6 done  
Copying blob 2cb8da93f979 done  
Copying blob 6517fb3ffeb2 done  
Copying config 96ae127d36 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
[sudo] password for my_local_username: 
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 2cb8da93f979 done  
Copying blob 7b3934b1b555 done  
Copying blob 2e4e862242a9 done  
Copying blob b12f34769c85 done  
Copying blob 9e0f46b6d29d done  
Copying blob ab61ffc4e8a6 done  
Copying blob 6517fb3ffeb2 done  
Copying config 96ae127d36 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Loaded image: localhost/verif_docker:latest
Error: exit status 125
```

Note that it randomly asked for the sudo password for my *local* account in the middle. First WTF, why does it need that? Second can it please *tell* me why it needs that?

Unfortunately it still failed with that `exit status 125` which is not a helpful message to put it mildly.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Create a local docker image.

Use the above `podman image scp` commands.

It may be relevant that $HOME on the remote machine is NFS.

### Describe the results you received

It didn't work.

### Describe the results you expected

It should have worked.

comments.body:
Last bit of the logs with debugging on:

```
Storing signatures
DEBU[0007] setting image creation date to 2023-01-12 11:22:08.130979692 +0000 UTC 
DEBU[0007] reusing image ID "96ae127d365f0ac585c2c2eec57f463dffc7eb97f57ffdb9d935b95bb4213d25" 
DEBU[0007] saved image metadata "{\"signatures-sizes\":{\"sha256:8cb942b1da5a7ec714133f330e73b45f152467581900e4ef1c8fb7e636496d4e\":[]}}" 
DEBU[0007] added name "localhost/verif_docker:latest" to image "96ae127d365f0ac585c2c2eec57f463dffc7eb97f57ffdb9d935b95bb4213d25" 
DEBU[0007] Called load.PersistentPostRunE(/usr/bin/podman --log-level=debug load --input /var/tmp/podman2393914383) 
Loaded image: localhost/verif_docker:latest
Error: exit status 125
```

Just to add to this I was attempting a podman image scp from a new machine and was getting an unhelpful exit status 255. I should point out I'm using `--ssh=native` and using an ed25519 key, as rsa isn't supported.

After hours of searching and attempting things, it turns out you need a config file in .ssh so this sorted it out:

```
touch ~/.ssh/config
chmod 600 ~/.ssh/config
```

If that helps anyone else. Some clearer error messages would be great though.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I'm experiencing the exact same issue as @Timmmm.

Any update on this?

I can provide more info to help try and debug this.
Honestly I ran into a situation where Podman didn't work and I needed Docker, so I've just installed that and it works.

@Timmmm I have been migrating from docker to podman, most of my workflow revolves around podman now. 

I have workaround with Ansible, but it's much more complicated that would be necessary if this issue could be resolved.

I decided to give this another try last week and managed to get it working. I re-read the docs and this [blog article](https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-transfer-container-images-without-registry) .

I setup the connection using `podman system connection add` and now it works flawlessly. I believe the first time I attempted this I wasn't setting the podman socket correctly.

From my point of view this issue can be closed.
